div1.entryFree:
Own, vb. 1) to acknowledge as one's property, to claim: two of these fellows you must know and o. Tp. V, 275. no father --ing it, Wint. III, 2, 89. a baboon, could he speak, would o. a name too dear, Per. IV, 6, 190.
2) to possess: move still, still so, and o. no other function, Wint. IV, 4, 143. not Afric --s a serpent I abhor more than thy fame and envy, Cor. I, 8, 3.
3) to confess: you will not o. it, Wint. III, 2, 60.
